title: ononis arvensis var. intermedia
intro: Ononis arvensis var. intermedia, also known as the field sainfoin, is a perennial plant in the legume family, characterized by its ability to fix nitrogen in the soil and its potential for use as a forage crop. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high content of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Ononis arvensis var. intermedia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared for herbal use in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
